title: Toll-like receptor 7 and/or 8 agonists and uses thereof
abstract: RNA oligonucleotide-based immunostimulatory Toll-Like Receptor 7 (TLR7) and/or TLR8 agonists are described. Also described are compositions comprising the TLR7 and/or TLR8 agonists, methods of making the TLR7 and/or TLR8 agonists, and methods of using the TLR7 and/or TLR8 agonists to treat immune diseases, disorders or conditions, such as viral infections or cancer.
claims:
Claims CLAIMS What is claimed is: 1. A TLR7 and/or TLR8 agonist having a formula of: (I) 5 â (P-L 1 -ssRNA-L2)m-A-T 3 â or (II) 5 â T-A-(L2-ssRNA-L 1 -P) m 3 â wherein: P independently represents a potentiating moiety selected from the group consisting of cholesterol, tocopherol, a palmitoyl group, and a stearyl group; ssRNA independently represents a single-stranded RNA having agonistic activity against TLR7 and/or TLR8; T is absent, or represents an asialoglycoprotein receptor targeting moiety or a centyrin; LI independently represents a bond or a first linker; L2 independently is absent, or represents a bond or a second linker; A is absent, or represents a bond or a nucleotide connected to L2 via a third linker; wherein each of the first linker, second linker and third linker comprise at least one atom selected from the group consisting of carbon, nitrogen, oxygen, sulfur, and phosphorus; and m is an integer from 1 to 3. 2. The TLR7 and/or TLR8 agonist of claim 1, having a structure of: (a) 5â P-L 1 -ssRNA-L2-A-T 3â or (b) 5â T-A-L2-ssR A-Ll-P 3â wherein LI comprises a phosphodiester (po) or phosphorothioate (ps) linkage, and comprises ((CH 2 ) 2 0)p, wherein p is an integer from 0 to 15. 3. The TLR7 and/or TLR8 agonist of claim 2, wherein: LI comprises po(HEG)po(HEG)po or ps(HEG)po(HEG)po, and HEG represents hexaethylene glycol ((CH2)20)6; and L2, A and T are each absent. 4. The TLR7 and/or TLR8 agonist of claim 1, having a structure of: wherein L2 independently comprises a phosphodiester (po) or a phosphorothioate (ps) linkage. 5. The TLR7 and/or TLR8 agonist of claim 4, wherein: L2 comprises a phosphorothioate (ps) bond; LI is a bond; T is absent; and A represents a nucleotide connected to L2 via a third linker. 6. The TLR7 and/or TLR8 agonist of claim 1, having a structure of: wherein L2 independently comprises a phosphodiester (po) or a phosphorothioate (ps) linkage, and comprises ((CH2)20) P , wherein p is an integer from 1 to 15. 7. The TLR7 and/or TLR8 agonist of claim 6, wherein: L2 comprises a phosphorothioate (ps) linkage, and comprises ((CH2)20)6; LI is a bond; T is absent; and A represents a nucleotide connected to L2 via a third linker. 8. The TLR7 and/or TLR8 agonist of any of claims 1 to 7, wherein: P independently represents cholesterol or tocopherol; and T is absent or represents an asialoglycoprotein receptor targeting moiety comprising one selected from the group consisting of galactose, galactosamine, N- formyl-galactosamine, N-acetylgalactosamine, N-propionyl-galactosamine, N-n- butanoyl-galactosamine, and N-isobutanoylgalactose-amine. 9. The TLR7 and/or TLR8 agonist of any of claims 1-8, wherein P represents cholesterol. 10. The TLR7 and/or TLR8 agonist of any of claims 1 to 9, wherein the single -stranded RNA has a phosphorothioate (ps) intemucleotide linkage. 11. The TLR7 and/or TLR8 agonist of any of claims 1 to 10, wherein the single -stranded RNA has 1 to 6 phosphodiester (po) intemucleotide linkages. 12. The TLR7 and/or TLR8 agonist of any of claims 1 to 11, wherein the single -stranded RNA has a length of 20 nucleotides. 13. The TLR7 and/or TLR8 agonist of any of claims 1 to 12, wherein the single -stranded RNA independently consists of a polynucleotide sequence selected from the group consisting of: SEQ ID NO : 1 5 â UUGUUGUUGUUGUUGUUGUU 3 â ; and SEQ ID NO: 2 5â UUUGGUUGUUUGUUUGUUUG 3 â . 14. The TLR7 and/or TLR8 agonist of claim 2, having a structure selected from the group consisting of: (1) 5â Toco-po(HEG)po(HEG)po- r(UUGUUGUUGUUGUUGUUGUU) 3â (SEQ ID NO: 1); (2) 5â Toco-po(HEG)po(HEG)po-r(UUUGGUUGUUUGUUUGUUUG) 3â (SEQ ID NO: 2); (3) 5âr(UUGUUGUUGUUGUUGUUGUU)(HEG)po(HEG)po-Toco 3â (SEQ ID NO: 1); (4) 5âr(UUUGGUUGUUUGUUUGUUUG)(HEG)po(HEG)po-Toco 3â (SEQ ID NO: 2); (5) 5 âChol-r(UUUGGUUGUUUGUUUGUUUG) 3â (SEQ ID NO: 2); (6) 5â Chol-r(UUGUUGUUGUUGUUG UUGUU) 3â (SEQ ID NO: 1); (7) 5 â Chol-po(HEG)po(HEG)po- r(UUGUUGUUGUUGUUGUUGUU) 3â (SEQ ID NO: 1); (8) 5 â Chol-po(HEG)po(HEG)po- r(UUUGGUUGUUUGUUUGUUUG) 3â (SEQ ID NO: 2); (9) 5âr(UUGUrUGUUGUUGUUGUUGUU)(HEG)po(HEG)po-Chol 3â (SEQ ID NO: 1); and (10) 5 âr(UUUGGUUGUUUGUUUGUUUG)(HEG)po(HEG)po-Chol 3 â (SEQ ID NO: 2) 

 wherein: Choi represents cholesterol; Toco represents tocopherol; HEG represents ((CEh)20)6; r represents ribose; po represents a phosphodiester linkage; ps represents a phosphorothioate linkage; and all of the intemucleotide linkages of the ssRNA are phosphorothioate (ps) linkages. 15. The TLR7 and/or TLR8 agonist of claim 4, having a structure of (SEQ ID NOS 2 and 2, respectively, in order of appearance): or (SEQ ID NOS 2 and 2, respectively, in order of appearance) wherein: Choi represents cholesterol; r represents ribose; and ps represents a phosphorothioate linkage. 16. The TLR7 and/or TLR8 agonist of claim 6, having a structure of (SEQ ID NOS 2, 2 and 2, respectively, in order of appearance): wherein: Choi represents cholesterol; HEG represents ((CH2)20)6; r represents ribose; and 

 ps represents a phosphorothioate linkage. 17. A pharmaceutical composition comprising a TLR7 and/or TLR8 agonist of any of claims 1-16 and a pharmaceutically acceptable carrier. 18. A method of preparing a pharmaceutical composition, comprising combining the TLR7 and/or TLR8 agonist of any of claims 1-16 with a pharmaceutically acceptable carrier. 19. A method of stimulating an immune response in a subject in need thereof, comprising administering to the subject the pharmaceutical composition of claim 17. 20. A method of treating a disease in a subject in need thereof, comprising administering to the subject the pharmaceutical composition of claim 17, wherein the disease is selected from the group consisting of a Hepatitis B infection and cancer.
